title: Ensure that the --wal-dir argument is set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --wal-dir argument is set as appropriate
input_row.description: Store etcd logs separately from etcd data.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should not be mixed with log data. Keeping the log data separate from the etcd data also ensures that those two types of data could individually be safeguarded. Also, you could use a centralized and remote log directory for persistent logging. Additionally, this separation also helps to avoid IO competition between logging and other IO operations.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the etcd server node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify that --wal-dir argument exists, and it is set as appropriate. At the minimum, it should not be set to the same directory as set for --data-dir argument.
input_row.remediation: Edit the etcd environment file (for example, /etc/etcd/etcd.conf ) on the etcd server node and set the ETCD_WAL_DIR parameter as appropriate: ETCD_WAL_DIR="<dir-name>" Edit the etcd startup file (for example, /etc/systemd/system/multi- user.target.wants/etcd.service ) and configure the startup parameter for --wal-dir and set it to \"${ETCD_WAL_DIR}\" : ExecStart=/bin/bash -c "GOMAXPROCS=$(nproc) /usr/bin/etcd --name=\"${ETCD_NAME}\" --data-dir=\"${ETCD_DATA_DIR}\" --listen-client-urls=\"${ETCD_LISTEN_CLIENT_URLS}\" --wal-dir=\"${ETCD_WAL_DIR}\"" Based on your system, reload the daemon and restart the etcd service. For example, systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart etcd.service 125 | P a g e Impact: None
input_row.default_value: By default, --wal-dir argument is not set.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the etcd server node.
2. Run the command: `ps -ef | grep etcd` to list the running etcd processes.
3. Verify that the `--wal-dir` argument exists in the output and check its value.
4. Ensure that the `--wal-dir` is not set to the same directory as the `--data-dir` argument.
5. If the `--wal-dir` is not set or is incorrectly configured, edit the etcd environment file (e.g., `/etc/etcd/etcd.conf`) and set the `ETCD_WAL_DIR` parameter appropriately: `ETCD_WAL_DIR="<dir-name>"`.
6. Edit the etcd startup file (e.g., `/etc/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/etcd.service`) to configure the startup parameter for `--wal-dir` to use the new `ETCD_WAL_DIR`: `ExecStart=/bin/bash -c "GOMAXPROCS=$(nproc) /usr/bin/etcd --name=\"${ETCD_NAME}\" --data-dir=\"${ETCD_DATA_DIR}\" --listen-client-urls=\"${ETCD_LISTEN_CLIENT_URLS}\" --wal-dir=\"${ETCD_WAL_DIR}\""`.
7. After making changes, reload the systemd daemon with `systemctl daemon-reload` and restart the etcd service with `systemctl restart etcd.service`.
8. Confirm that the changes have been applied by re-running the command from step 2 and verifying the `--wal-dir` argument.
